title: salix lasiandra var. macrophylla
intro: Salix lasiandra var. macrophylla, also known as the Pacific willow, is a large shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The Pacific willow has various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The bark and leaves of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
